title: Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce

seo.title: How to Make Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce — Easy Mexican Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce. This easy Mexican recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
seo.mainKeyword: Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce
longTailKeywords: how to make Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce
easy Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce recipe
authentic Mexican Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Mexican Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce — a beloved Mexican classic

description: Pollo en Mole is a truly iconic Mexican dish, featuring tender chicken bathed in a rich, complex, and deeply aromatic mole sauce. This traditional recipe showcases a masterful blend of chiles, spices, nuts, seeds, and a touch of chocolate, creating a symphony of flavors. It is a comforting and celebratory meal that embodies the heart of Mexican culinary heritage.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Season the chicken pieces generously with salt. In a large pot, add 2 cups of chicken broth and bring to a simmer. Add the chicken and cook for about 20-25 minutes, or until cooked through. Remove the chicken from the broth and set aside, reserving the cooking liquid.

instructions.title: Rehydrate the Chiles
instructions.text: Heat a dry comal or skillet over medium heat. Toast the dried ancho, mulato, and pasilla chiles for 1-2 minutes per side until fragrant. Be careful not to burn them. Transfer the toasted chiles to a bowl, cover with hot water, and let them soak for at least 20 minutes until softened. Drain and reserve the soaking water.

instructions.title: Toast Nuts, Seeds, and Spices
instructions.text: In the same dry skillet, toast the almonds, sesame seeds, pumpkin seeds, and anise seeds until lightly golden and fragrant. Remove and set aside. Briefly toast the torn corn tortilla pieces until slightly crisp. Set aside.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: Add 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the skillet. Sauté the chopped onion, garlic, and tomato until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.

instructions.title: Blend the Mole Base
instructions.text: Combine the rehydrated chiles, toasted nuts, seeds, tortilla pieces, sautéed aromatics, raisins, ground cinnamon, and ground cloves in a blender. Add 1 cup of the reserved chicken broth and blend until very smooth. If the mixture is too thick, add a little more chicken broth or chile soaking water as needed.

instructions.title: Simmer the Mole Sauce
instructions.text: Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Carefully pour the blended mole mixture into the pot, stirring constantly to prevent sticking. Add the Mexican chocolate and stir until it melts and is fully incorporated. Continue to simmer the mole sauce for at least 30-40 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it thickens and the flavors meld. Season with salt to taste.

instructions.title: Combine and Serve
instructions.text: Add the cooked chicken pieces to the mole sauce and gently stir to coat completely. Simmer for another 10-15 minutes to allow the chicken to absorb the rich flavors of the mole. Serve hot, garnished with extra sesame seeds. This dish is excellent with white rice or warm tortillas.

tags: Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce
tips: For a truly authentic mole, toast your chiles and spices carefully. This step is crucial for developing deep, complex flavors. Do not burn them, as this will make the sauce bitter.
Mexican chocolate is key for that characteristic depth and subtle sweetness. If unavailable, use unsweetened dark chocolate with a pinch of sugar.
Mole sauce benefits greatly from simmering. The longer it simmers, the more the flavors meld and deepen. You can even make the sauce a day ahead; it tastes even better the next day.
article: Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce is a beloved Mexican Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
